pattern_recognition: Interval Merging Problem - This problem falls under the category of interval problems where we need to handle overlapping intervals. The key signal is the presence of overlapping ranges that need to be combined into a single range.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Check every pair of intervals to see if they overlap and merge them accordingly.
approaches.algorithm: Iterate through each interval and compare it with every other interval to find overlaps, merging them as needed.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Sort the intervals by their start times and then iterate through them, merging overlapping intervals in a single pass.
approaches.algorithm: Sort the intervals, then use a single loop to merge overlapping intervals by comparing the current interval with the last merged interval.

dry_run: Consider the input intervals: [[1, 3], [2, 6], [8, 10], [15, 18]]. After sorting, we have the same order. Start with the first interval [1, 3]. Compare it with [2, 6]; they overlap, so merge to [1, 6]. Next, [8, 10] does not overlap with [1, 6], so add it as is. Finally, [15, 18] also does not overlap, so add it. The result is [[1, 6], [8, 10], [15, 18]].
edge_cases: Empty input should return an empty array. A single interval should return itself. Duplicate intervals should be merged correctly. Large inputs should be handled efficiently without exceeding time limits. Overflow should be considered for very large interval values.
common_pitfalls: Common mistakes include: 1) Not sorting the intervals before merging, leading to incorrect results. 2) Failing to handle cases where intervals touch but do not overlap. 3) Incorrectly merging intervals that do not actually overlap, resulting in lost data.
